The development of cardiac hypertrophy and failure can be monitored using noninvasive imaging with echocardiogram in conscious animals.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7790i","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4rrmd","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2ni0i","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1rq45","text":"Modified from: Timofeyev V. et al. J Mol Cell Cardiol. 2006 Jul;41(1):170-81.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: The animals’ chest will be shaved and then be placed in a supine position on a warmed (37°C) towel covered surgical table.
The mice are restrained in a 50 ml conical tube with an opening for the nose and chest, a standard procedure for imaging mice for this kind of study.
The imaging generally lasts for only 2-5 min, we were trained in this method by CLAS personnel and in our experience works very well.
M-mode and two-dimensional measurements are used to assess systolic function.
An average of six selected cardiac cycles from at least two separate scans performed in randomblindfashion with papillary muscles used as a point of reference.
End diastole was defined as the maximal left ventricular (LV) diastolic dimension and end systole was defined as the peak of posterior wall motion.

Fractional shortening (FS), a surrogate of systolic function, was calculated from LV dimensions as follows: FS = ((EDD − ESD) / EDD) × 100%, where EDD and ESD are LV end diastolic and end systolic dimension, respectively.
creator: Lili Liang
